cards.text: POCA detention orders run 2 years and renew indefinitely — and the 2014 amendment removed judicial review of the appointed Board's decisions.
cards.sub: Detainees enter via 60-day police investigation periods (24h initial + 21-day + 38-day extensions) before the Prevention Board hearing.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: POCA 2014 amendment removed judicial review of Prevention Board decisions — the Board's reasoning is not subject to High Court scrutiny.
cards.s: Board members are appointed by the Yang di-Pertuan Agong on the minister's advice. There is no public reasoning standard.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Article 5 protects personal liberty — POCA operates as a constitutional carve-out under Article 149.
cards.s: Article 149 specifically enables Parliament to enact preventive-detention laws notwithstanding other liberty protections.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Civil-society reports indicate POCA detainees are disproportionately drawn from lower-income communities — though disaggregated official data is not published.
cards.s: The asymmetry between civil-society reporting and official disclosure is itself a structural transparency gap.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: POCA is not an emergency power that has been reluctantly retained — it is an active, routine detention regime, just one without a courtroom.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Like a prison sentence decided by an appointed committee — same outcome, no courtroom, no published reasoning.
cards.sub: Preventive detention achieves imprisonment without the constitutional safeguards of a criminal trial.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: POCA reform requires either restoring judicial review of Board decisions or publishing disaggregated outcome data — preferably both.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: POCA Allows 2-Year Renewable Detention With No Judicial Review — Decided by an Appointed Board
